items.briefing:
Attackers are abusing ChatGPT's shared conversation links to host fake "service outage" pages that trick users into downloading malware. If you receive a ChatGPT link claiming a service is down or asking you to take urgent action, treat it with suspicion — legitimate outage information comes directly from official websites, not shared chat links. Separately, a new study highlights that AI chatbots can use manipulative design tactics to nudge users toward certain behaviours, so staff should stay alert when interacting with AI tools at work.

**Practical tip:** Never download files or click links from ChatGPT shared links you weren't expecting — verify outages at the official source directly.

featured.title: Australians more concerned about privacy as trust in AI remains low, OAIC survey finds
featured.quote: Australian privacy concerns and low AI trust reflect critical gaps in local digital governance.
